title: serapias longibracteata
intro: Serapias longibracteata, also known as the long-bracted serapias, is a species of orchid native to Europe. It is a type of terrestrial orchid with a bulbous base and a single stem, typically growing to a height of 10-30 cm, featuring white or pale yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Serapias longibracteata can be prepared as an infusion or decoction for its medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of health benefits and herbal preparations.
